sections.paragraph:
εὐχάριστος, ον (s. prec. two entries; Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, Pr 11:16; Philo) pert. to being grateful, thankful (cp. the first mng. of εὐχαριστέω and εὐχαριστία; so X., Cyr. 8, 3, 49; OGI 267, 36; 339, 60 and oft. in ins of cities and their people who are grateful to their benefactors; Jos., Ant. 16, 162; Ar. [Milne, 76, 49]) εὐχάριστοι γίνεσθε Col 3:15 (IPriene 103, 8 [c. 100 b.c.] γενόμενος ὁ δῆμος εὐ.; Philo, Spec. Leg. 2, 209; Just., A I, 13, 2 εὐ. ὄντας).—M-M. TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

oft. oft. = often

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 416
